title: Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive
description: url: http://example.com # Sensitive - name: HTTPS request url: https://example.com AWS Documentation - Listeners for your Application Load Balancers AWS Documentation - Stream Encryption Articles & blog posts Google - Moving towards more secure web Mozilla - Deprecating non secure http CWE - CWE-200 - Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor CWE - CWE-319 - Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information STIG Viewer - Application Security and Development: V-222397 - The application must implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of remote access sessions. STIG Viewer - Application Security and Development: V-222534 - Service-Oriented Applications handling non-releasable data must authenticate endpoint devices via mutual SSL/TLS. STIG Viewer - Application Security and Development: V-222562 - Applications used for non-local maintenance must implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of maintenance and diagnostic communications.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Clear-text protocol detected (HTTP/FTP) - use HTTPS/FTPS instead
Insecure protocol module detected (telnet/ftp) - use SSH/SFTP instead
Using insecure protocol module - consider SSH/SFTP alternatives
Clear-text HTTP usage detected in commands or environment
Clear-text HTTP URL in variable assignment
